requirements.name: Context of the organisation
requirements.description: The organisation must understand its internal and external context, identify interested parties and their requirements, and define the scope of the ISMS. This forms the foundation for the entire information security management system.

requirements.name: Leadership and commitment
requirements.description: Top management must demonstrate leadership and commitment by establishing an information security policy, assigning roles and responsibilities, and ensuring integration of ISMS requirements into business processes. An information security policy must be documented and communicated.

requirements.name: Risk assessment and treatment
requirements.description: The organisation must define and apply an information security risk assessment process: identifying risks, analysing and evaluating risk levels, selecting risk treatment options (mitigate, accept, avoid, transfer), and implementing a risk treatment plan.

requirements.name: Support – Resources, competence, awareness
requirements.description: The organisation must determine and provide necessary resources, ensure people are competent (education, training, experience), create awareness of the policy and their contribution, and establish a documented information process (documentation control).

requirements.name: Operational planning and control
requirements.description: The organisation must plan, implement, control, and review processes to meet ISMS requirements. This includes operational risk assessment and treatment processes, change management, and controls over outsourced processes.

requirements.name: Performance evaluation
requirements.description: The organisation must monitor, measure, analyse and evaluate the ISMS, conduct internal audits at planned intervals, and carry out management reviews. Results must be documented.

requirements.name: Improvement – Nonconformity and corrective action
requirements.description: When a nonconformity occurs, the organisation must react to it, evaluate causes, implement corrective actions, and review their effectiveness. Opportunities for continual improvement must be identified.

requirements.name: Annex A – Organisational controls
requirements.description: Key controls include: information security policies (A.5.1), roles and responsibilities (A.5.2), threat intelligence (A.5.7), information security in project management (A.5.8), asset management (A.5.9), acceptable use (A.5.10), information classification (A.5.12), handling of classified information (A.5.13), supplier relationships and ICT supply chain (A.5.19-5.22), incident management (A.5.24-5.28), business continuity (A.5.29-5.30), legal and compliance (A.5.31-5.36).

requirements.name: Annex A – People controls
requirements.description: Controls related to personnel: background screening (A.6.1), terms and conditions of employment (A.6.2), information security awareness and training (A.6.3), disciplinary process (A.6.4), responsibilities after termination (A.6.5), remote working (A.6.7), information security event reporting (A.6.8).

requirements.name: Annex A – Physical controls
requirements.description: Controls for physical and environmental security: physical security perimeters (A.7.1), physical entry (A.7.2), securing offices and facilities (A.7.3), clear desk and clear screen (A.7.7), equipment siting and protection (A.7.8), secure disposal of equipment (A.7.14).

requirements.name: Annex A – Technical controls: Access & Authentication
requirements.description: Controls covering identity and access management: user endpoint devices (A.8.1), privileged access rights (A.8.2), information access restriction (A.8.3), authentication (A.8.5) including multi-factor authentication (MFA), capacity management (A.8.6), malware protection (A.8.7), web filtering (A.8.23), data masking (A.8.11), data leakage prevention (A.8.12).

requirements.name: Annex A – Technical controls: Vulnerability & Secure Development
requirements.description: Controls for managing technical vulnerabilities and secure software development: technical vulnerability management and patching (A.8.8), configuration management (A.8.9), source code management (A.8.4), secure development lifecycle (A.8.25-8.31), change management for systems, and use of privileged utility programs (A.8.18).

requirements.name: Annex A – Technical controls: Monitoring, Backup & Encryption
requirements.description: Controls for detecting incidents and ensuring resilience: logging and monitoring (A.8.15-8.16), clock synchronisation (A.8.17), backup (A.8.13), redundancy and availability (A.8.14), encryption (A.8.24), network security and segmentation (A.8.20-8.22).
